column1: # Oni Hunter — staging cards (merge script strips comment lines)
# L1 Core additions (3) — reaction, defense, passive to round out the 12-card L1 pool

column3: Reaction: when an undying or Between-entity enters your adjacent space, it halts and its next manifestation check suffers — the old wards still bite.
Defense: intercept an incoming attack from a marked quarry; the blow slides along your edge and cuts a sliver of its anchoring thread free.
Passive: you perceive resonance bleed the way others see smoke. Undying, shell-state, and Between-touched foes cannot remain hidden from you at close range.
Level-up: you may now carry a second Hunter's Mark at once. Splitting your attention costs you none of the bonus against either quarry.
Level-up: your blade is scored with resonance-grooves. Once per round, when you hit a marked quarry, you may spend 0 AP to apply a minor dissolution rider to the strike.
Enter a sustained hunt-state against a single quarry. Each round the hunt persists, your strikes against the quarry grow heavier; break line-of-sight for a full round and the hunt ends.
A strike placed precisely where the quarry's form is weakest-bound. The wound does not bleed; it unknits, and refuses to close on its own.
Against a marked quarry, seize a visible resonance-thread and drag. The target staggers toward you and its next manifestation check is disrupted.
Apply a dissolution reagent to an open unmaking wound. The target cannot heal or re-form that wound until the reagent burns out.
Reaction: when a quarry with unmaking wounds drops below its final threshold, speak the closing phrase — the target cannot be stabilized, summoned-back, or resurrected this scene.
A short ritual cut, woven mid-combat. The quarry's true-name anchor is severed; no ally of yours counts it as a returning foe, and the dead stay dead this scene.

Pour dissolution-resonance into the quarry until its outline shivers. For the rest of the scene the target is treated as a form barely holding itself together — every unmaking effect against it hits harder.
Drive a resonance-marked spike into the ground beneath a downed foe. While the spike holds, that corpse cannot be re-animated, possessed, or called back.
Draw a line of ash between you and a re-manifesting spirit. The target cannot cross, and each attempt to cross drains more of its anchoring reserve.
Recite a short verse over a spirit or shell-state entity. It becomes fixed in place; any movement or shift through Between-space requires it to break the verse first.
Reaction: when an enemy would re-manifest, speak its last rites instead. It finishes dying properly, and any tether it had to the world frays inert.
Consecrate the battlefield for the rest of the scene. Nothing can be summoned, raised, or re-manifested inside its bounds; existing undying suffer ongoing anchor-strain.
You speak a true-name into the earth and close it over. The named entity cannot return to this scene, nor to the next, and allies who knew it forget it was ever a threat.
On felling an undying or Between-entity, siphon a sliver of its essence into a reserve vial. You gain a stored Essence charge.
Crack an Essence vial over your next action. Spend 1 Essence charge to fuel a card this turn as if you had spent 1 additional AP on it.
Coat your weapon with harvested oni-essence. Your next strike this turn hits the target's specific kind of weakness, as if cut from its own stolen heart.
Hurl a charged Essence vial at a point. It bursts in a resonance-plume; enemies caught within suffer disruption to their next enhancement or manifestation check.
Open your collected reserve all at once. For the rest of the scene, you may spend Essence charges in place of AP on any Oni Hunter card, one charge per AP.
Drink down a stored oni-essence. You take on a fraction of its form this scene — your strikes carry its signature, and enemies of its kind recognize you as one of their own long enough to hesitate.
